data.title: Lifecore Biomedical receives accelerated payment of remaining $10M in proceeds from equipment sale
data.description:
• Lifecore Biomedical (NASDAQ:LFCR) has received the remaining $10 million in proceeds from the previously disclosed sale of its excess high-speed, multi-purpose 10-head isolator filler.
  • The buyer elected to complete the full payment ahead of schedule, opting to deliver the remaining balance as a lump sum rather than over the originally agreed 18-month installment plan.
  • Lifecore (NASDAQ:LFCR) had previously received $7 million at the close of the $17 million transaction in early 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: BE Semiconductor ups financial targets at investor day
data.description:
BE Semiconductor (OTC:BESIY) upped its financial targets at its investor day on Thursday, citing strength in its tools for advanced chip stacking.

“Besi management conducted a detailed strategic plan review in conjunction with key customers and other stakeholders in March of this year,” Richard W. Blickman, President and Chief Executive Officer of Besi, said in a statement. “The review highlighted the expanded deployment of AI technologies in data center, edge computing and consumer applications through 2030. It also highlighted the accelerated adoption of 2.5D and 3D chiplet-based, wafer level assembly structures in our principal end user markets as data volumes increase significantly while Moore’s Law slows. This, in turn, should drive higher expected demand for Besi’s advanced packaging solutions for both logic and memory applications.”

Blickman continued: “Accordingly, we are raising Besi’s long-term target revenue forecast including forecasts for our sub micron die attach systems and AI related mainstream die attach systems which account for a significant portion of our increased revenue, gross margin and operating margin targets.”

BE Semiconductor, also known as Besi, now expects 2025 revenue to be between €1.5B and €1.9B, up from a previous forecast of €1B or more, which was provided at last year's investor day. The company also upped its forecast for gross margins and operating margins. It now sees the metrics between 64% and 68% and 40% and 55%, compared to its previous forecast of 62% to 66% and 35% to 50%, respectively.

Based in the Netherlands, Besi produces a hybrid bonding tool that allows processors to be bonded on top of one another.

data.title: Cardinal Health raises FY25 earnings outlook on investor day
data.description:
Shares of Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) traded higher in the premarket on Thursday after the medical distributor, in conjunction with its Investor Day, raised its full-year earnings outlook and set its long-term financial goals.

Dublin, Ohio-based Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) said it currently expects its non-GAAP diluted earnings for FY25 to reach $8.15 - $8.20 per share, up from the prior forecast of $8.05 - $8.15 and just above $8.13 in the consensus.

The company also issued a preliminary estimate of $9.10 - $9.30 as its FY26 non-GAAP diluted EPS compared to $9.13 per share in the consensus, implying ~13% YoY growth at the midpoint.

"We are proud of the strong and resilient foundation we have built that enables us to deliver compelling future growth," said CEO Jason Hollar ahead of the company’s investor day scheduled to begin at 9:00 a.m. ET on Thursday.

Cardinal Health (NYSE:CAH) reaffirmed its long-term goal to grow its non-GAAP diluted EPS at 12% - 14% CAGR for FY26 - FY28. Additionally, the company intends to achieve 8% - 10% and 2% - 4% CAGR of revenue growth in its Pharmaceutical & Specialty Solutions and Global Medical Products & Distribution segments from FY26 to FY28, respectively.

Other highlights of CAH's investor day include the company's plans to increase annual baseline share repurchases for the next three years by $250M to at least $750M.

data.title: UMB Financial to redeem series A preferred stock outstanding
data.description:
UMB Financial (NASDAQ:UMBF) announced on Thursday a redemption of the outstanding shares of its 7.00% fixed-rate reset non-cumulative perpetual preferred stock, series A.

The 11,500 outstanding shares will be redeemed at $10,000 per share on the dividend payment date of July 15.

data.title: Bunge nears China ruling on $8.2B Viterra deal - Bloomberg
data.description:
Bunge (NYSE:BG) is close to receiving a decision from Chinese antitrust authorities on its $8.2B acquisition of Viterra, roughly two years after the deal was first announced, Bloomberg reported late Wednesday.

China's approval is the last major hurdle in Bunge's (NYSE:BG) bid to create an agricultural commodities giant capable of competing with Cargill, and a verdict could come within days, according to the report.

The deal already received key approvals in the European Union and Canada, and Argentina's antitrust laws allow for the deal to be completed, with any remedial action potentially being required later.

Bunge's (BG) attempt to expand with the purchase of rival Viterra has been caught up in trade tensions between the U.S. and China, and the latest movement comes alongside the recent trade negotiations in London.

Bunge (BG) initially planned to close the deal by mid-2024, and is approaching a Friday deadline which allows the parties to pull out of the agreement, but the limit reportedly has been extended.

data.title: Karooooo CEO prices $75M share offering at $50 a piece
data.description:
• Karooooo (NASDAQ:KARO) has announced the pricing of an underwritten secondary public offering of 1.5M ordinary shares held by its CEO Isaias (Zak) Jose Calisto.
  • The shares are being sold at a price to the public of $50.00 per ordinary share for total gross proceeds of ~$75M. In addition, the underwriters will have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 225,000 ordinary shares at the public offering price.
  • The sale is expected to close on June 13, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Following completion of the offering, Calisto will continue to beneficially own 17,917,958 ordinary shares, or 58.00% (or 17,692,958 ordinary shares, or 57.27% if the option to purchase additional shares is exercised in full) of the 30,893,300 ordinary shares issued and outstanding.
  • KARO -14.3% premarket.

data.title: Senmiao Technology and Qinhong International sign partnership to promote services for SMEs in China
data.description:
• Senmiao Technology (NASDAQ:AIHS) Thursday said that it has signed a strategic cooperation agreement with Qinhong International Group.
  • Since its incorporation in 2013, Qinhong has provided financing facilitation support, financial services referral, and loan consulting services, primarily to small and medium-sized enterprises (“SMEs”) and other financial institutions in Southwest China. 
  • Under the agreement, leveraging both companies’ existing business relationships and customer resources, Senmiao (NASDAQ:AIHS) will have the opportunity to participate in Qinhong’s business across China.
  • Furthermore, Qinhong, relying on its extensive relationships with banks and other financial institutions, will assist Senmiao in securing financing to fund its business plan when needed.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Luca Mining COO Ramon Mendoza sheds interim tag
data.description:
Luca Mining (OTCQX:LUCMF) announces that Ramon Mendoza, currently Luca's interim COO, will assume the position of permanent COO effective as of June 01, 2025. He will also continue to be the company's Chief Technical Officer.

Mendoza has over 35 years of experience in the mining industry, focusing on mine development and process optimization. He successfully improved gold and metal production at Luca's Campo Morado and Tahuehueto mines in Mexico.

The company also announces that David Rhodes will be stepping down as Chairman effective as of June 10, 2025, however he will remain a valued member of Luca's board.

Peter Damouni will step into the role of Chairman of the Board. He has served as an executive and director of several public companies listed on the TSX, TSXV, and LSE.

data.title: Sachem Capital announces private placement of $100M senior secured notes
data.description:
Real estate lender Sachem Capital (NYSE:SACH) announced on Thursday a private placement of $100M five-year senior secured notes due June 11, 2030, to various institutional investors.

The company said an initial draw of $50M was made at closing, and the remaining $50M must be drawn by May 15, 2026.

The notes carry an interest at a fixed rate of 9.875% per annum, payable quarterly, and a commitment fee of 1.0% on the undrawn portion.

The payment due on the notes are fully and unconditionally guaranteed by the company.

The proceeds are intended to be used for a combination of purposes, including the repayment of existing facility balances, the origination of new investments, and to redeem the 7.75% unsecured notes maturing in September.

data.title: ADC Therapeutics announces $100M private financing
data.description:
• ADC Therapeutics (NYSE:ADCT) has entered into securities purchase agreements for the sale of its equity securities to certain institutional investors in a $100M private investment in public equity ("PIPE”) financing.
  • The company is selling 13M common shares at $3.53 a share and pre-funded warrants to purchase 15.7M common shares at $3.43 per pre-funded warrant.
  • Gross proceeds from the financing are anticipated to be ~$100M, extending the company’s expected cash runway into 2028.
  • ADC Therapeutics plans to use the net proceeds to fund multiple catalysts primarily in support of ZYNLONTA clinical development as well as ZYNLONTA commercialization activities, working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The PIPE is expected to close on June 16, 2025, subject to customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release
